content:
Another significant milestone today.

A highly contested writ petition filed by Vedanta against our client, the Directorate General of Hydrocarbons (DGH), came to be dismissed by the Hon’ble Delhi High Court.

The matter was remarkable in many ways. At the notice stage itself, the Court delivered a detailed judgment spanning nearly 100 pages, reflecting the complexity and importance of the issues involved.

Working on this case has been one of the most enriching experiences of my professional journey. We had countless conferences with the Learned Attorney General across the period of 10 months for and the briefing notes prepared by Shrinkhla Tiwari were truly exhaustive and played pivotal role.

For me, the greatest takeaway was the opportunity to discuss and work on the Public Trust Doctrine in depth with Ld Attorney General, particularly its application to contracts concerning sovereign natural resources. Watching these principles being debated and applied in a matter of such significance was an invaluable learning experience.

Grateful to have had the opportunity to be part of this matter.
comment: An interesting dimension is how such rulings may influence future resource contracts—balancing investor certainty with the State’s continuing fiduciary obligations over public assets.
